summary: Israel has intensified its air campaign in southern Lebanon, striking over 80 Hezbollah targets and reporting multiple soldier casualties. Hezbollah continues rocket and drone attacks on Israel despite a tentative ceasefire. Diplomatic talks between Iran and the US were postponed due to the Israeli strikes.
key_points: The Israeli Air Force has targeted more than 80 Hezbollah positions in southern Lebanon, killing dozens of militants (Source: @almogboker78/17515).
Hezbollah launched 63 rockets, 1 missile, 7 explosive UAVs, 2 IED attacks, and a terrorist infiltration involving 5 operatives between June 17-18 (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Four Israeli soldiers were killed in a drone strike on a tank in Kfar Tebnit, with additional injuries to five soldiers (Source: @manniefabian/58628).
Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a ceasefire to begin at 4 p.m. Beirut time, according to a senior US official (Source: @manniefabian/58633).
Iranian Foreign Ministry announced a 60‑day suspension of maritime passage fees for vessels (Source: @N12chat/213682).
Talks between Iran and the US in Switzerland were postponed because of the Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4120).
U.S. officials reported that a ceasefire is set to take effect at 4 a.m. Beirut time (Source: @naya_foriraq/79293).

sections.executive_summary: Israel has intensified its air campaign in southern Lebanon, striking over 80 Hezbollah targets and reporting multiple soldier casualties. Hezbollah continues to launch rockets, missiles, and drones against Israel despite a tentative ceasefire. Diplomatic negotiations involving Iran and the United States have been delayed due to the escalation.

points: Iranian Foreign Ministry announced that for the next 60 days no passage fees will be levied on ships, signaling a move to facilitate maritime traffic (Source: @N12chat/213682).
David Yaghoubian stated that Iran now has a historic opportunity to lead a new petrodollar configuration in the Persian Gulf (Source: @presstv/194756).
Iran warned that it will take all necessary steps to protect its interests and allies after condemning Israeli strikes in Lebanon (Source: @N12chat/213696).
Talks between Iran and the United States in Switzerland were postponed because of Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon, according to three sources (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4120).
The Israeli Defense Forces reported more than 150 airstrikes in Lebanon since midnight, targeting Hezbollah infrastructure (Source: @idf_telegram/24395).
Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered a strong response to Hezbollah violations, stating that Israel will continue operations in southern Lebanon as needed (Source: @almogboker78/17515).
Four Israeli soldiers were killed when an explosive drone hit a tank in Kfar Tebnit, with additional injuries to five personnel (Source: @manniefabian/58628).
The IDF announced that it has struck Hezbollah command posts in the Bakaa area and destroyed launch positions across southern Lebanon (Source: @inon_yttach/9065).
A senior US official confirmed that Israel and Hezbollah have agreed to a ceasefire starting at 4 p.m. Beirut time (Source: @manniefabian/58633).
Israeli airstrikes hit the towns of Shoukin and Zebdine in southern Lebanon (Source: @presstv/194757).
Hezbollah carried out 25 separate attacks, including 63 rockets and 7 explosive UAVs, against Israel between June 17‑18 (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Four Israeli soldiers were killed and five wounded in a drone attack on a tank in Kfar Tebnit, southern Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58628).
Hezbollah continued violations of the ceasefire, prompting IDF strikes on over 80 targets in the Bakaa and Nabatieh areas (Source: @inon_yttach/9065).
Lebanese President stated that Israeli attacks will not prevent a ceasefire from being achieved (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74021).

key_developments: Israel has launched over 80 airstrikes on Hezbollah positions in southern Lebanon, killing dozens of militants and causing multiple Israeli casualties (Source: @almogboker78/17515).
Hezbollah executed 63 rockets, 1 missile, and 7 explosive UAV attacks against Israel in a 48‑hour period (Source: @shin_persian/7128).
Four Israeli soldiers were killed and five wounded by an explosive drone in Kfar Tebnit, highlighting the threat of Hezbollah UAVs (Source: @manniefabian/58628).
A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is set to begin at 4 p.m. Beirut time, according to a senior US official (Source: @manniefabian/58633).
Iran announced a 60‑day suspension of maritime passage fees, indicating a strategic economic move in the region (Source: @N12chat/213682).
Talks between Iran and the United States in Switzerland were postponed due to the Israeli air campaign in Lebanon (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4120).
U.S. officials reported that the ceasefire will be implemented at 4 a.m. Beirut time, confirming the timeline for de‑escalation (Source: @naya_foriraq/79293).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah remains capable of launching rockets, missiles, and explosive drones despite ceasefire negotiations, posing a persistent threat to northern Israel. Israeli airpower continues to target Hezbollah command and launch infrastructure, indicating a high-intensity conflict in southern Lebanon. Iranian diplomatic and economic actions suggest a broader regional dimension, potentially influencing the conflict's trajectory.
sections.regional_response: The United States has signaled support for a ceasefire while postponing diplomatic talks with Iran due to Israeli operations. Iran has warned of taking necessary steps to protect its interests and allies, reflecting heightened regional tension. Lebanese authorities acknowledge Israeli strikes but assert that they will not derail ceasefire efforts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah UAV activity is critical, as recent attacks have caused Israeli casualties. Continued Israeli airstrikes on Bakaa and Nabatieh command posts suggest a focus on degrading Hezbollah's command‑and‑control. The suspension of Iranian maritime fees may affect logistics and supply routes for regional actors. Diplomatic channels remain fragile, with US‑Iran talks delayed by battlefield developments.
